title: calamintha nepeta var. glandulosa
intro: Calamintha nepeta var. glandulosa, also known as glandular calamint, is a subspecies of the Calamintha nepeta plant, a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. This herb has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of essential oils. The health benefits of Calamintha nepeta var. glandulosa include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Calamintha nepeta var. glandulosa can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or topical application, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
